---
title: "Panel Data"
subtitle: "Exploiting repeated observations to control for the unobserved"
---

## Overview

Panel data --- repeated observations on the same units over time --- offer a powerful strategy for causal inference. By comparing a unit to itself across time, fixed effects methods can absorb time-invariant unobserved heterogeneity that would otherwise confound our estimates.

This module introduces panel data structures, the mechanics and interpretation of fixed effects, and the practical issues that arise in real panel analyses.

## Pages in This Module

- [Panel Structure](panel-structure.qmd) --- What panel data look like and why they matter.
- [Fixed Effects](fixed-effects.qmd) --- Within-unit estimation and the demean operator.
- [Practical Issues](practical-issues.qmd) --- Serial correlation, clustering, and unbalanced panels.
